label: test_coverage_gap
summary: Core sliding-window algorithm is directionally correct: timestamps are filtered per request, the default 100/min limit fires on the 101st request (not the 100th — off-by-one is in the right direction), and XFF parsing takes the leftmost value as expected. Two blocking gaps prevent merge. First: the hits Map leaks memory because IP keys are never removed — the per-request filter only trims the timestamp array for active IPs; dormant IPs accumulate indefinitely. Second: only 1 of 6 mandated test scenarios was written; at-limit, over-limit, proxy header, and cleanup cases are all missing. The XFF spoofing risk is a warning, not a blocker given the criterion's wording, but should be acknowledged. Fix the Map cleanup and add the five missing tests, then re-review.

findings.description: IP entries in the hits Map are never deleted — an IP that stops making requests leaves its entry in the Map indefinitely, causing unbounded memory growth with enough unique source IPs (e.g. a scanner or rotating proxies).
findings.suggestion: After filtering, if recent.length === 0 call hits.delete(ip) to prune dead entries, or add a periodic setInterval cleanup that deletes any Map entry whose most recent timestamp is older than windowMs.

findings.description: Only 1 of 6 required test scenarios is present (under-limit); at-limit, over-limit (429 response), X-Forwarded-For header, and cleanup are all absent.
findings.suggestion: Add: a test asserting next() is called on the Nth request (at-limit), a test asserting res.status(429) on the (N+1)th request (over-limit), a test with req.headers['x-forwarded-for'] set to verify IP extraction, and a test that advances time past windowMs and confirms the counter resets (cleanup).

findings.description: X-Forwarded-For is trusted unconditionally — any client can set this header to an arbitrary IP value and bypass rate limiting entirely.
findings.suggestion: Document the assumption that a trusted reverse proxy is always present, or add a trustProxy option that disables XFF parsing when false (matching Express's own trust proxy semantics).

findings.description: req, res, and next parameters have implicit any types; TypeScript cannot catch misuse of the returned middleware.
findings.suggestion: Type parameters as Express.Request, Express.Response, NextFunction or equivalent.

fullReview.summary: Core sliding-window algorithm is directionally correct: timestamps are filtered per request, the default 100/min limit fires on the 101st request (not the 100th — off-by-one is in the right direction), and XFF parsing takes the leftmost value as expected. Two blocking gaps prevent merge. First: the hits Map leaks memory because IP keys are never removed — the per-request filter only trims the timestamp array for active IPs; dormant IPs accumulate indefinitely. Second: only 1 of 6 mandated test scenarios was written; at-limit, over-limit, proxy header, and cleanup cases are all missing. The XFF spoofing risk is a warning, not a blocker given the criterion's wording, but should be acknowledged. Fix the Map cleanup and add the five missing tests, then re-review.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Middleware rejects with 429 when limit exceeded
criteriaAssessment.evidence: rateLimit.ts line 15: `if (recent.length > limit) return res.status(429).json(...)`. Logic is correct — push happens before check, so the (limit+1)th request triggers 429.
Map<string, number[]> keyed by IP; filter `t > now - windowMs` on every request produces a true sliding window.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Default limit: 100/minute, configurable via middleware options
criteriaAssessment.evidence: `opts.limit ?? 100` and `opts.windowMs ?? 60_000` in rateLimit.ts lines 3-4. RateLimitOptions interface exposes both fields.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Correctly handles X-Forwarded-For header when behind proxy
criteriaAssessment.evidence: Line 9: `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']?.split(',')[0] ?? req.ip` extracts the leftmost (client) IP from XFF and falls back to req.ip. Functional but unconditionally trusts the client-supplied header.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Cleans up expired entries to prevent memory leak
criteriaAssessment.evidence: Timestamp arrays are trimmed per request via filter, but the Map entry for each IP is never deleted. An IP seen once and never again retains its (eventually-empty) entry forever. High-cardinality IP sets cause unbounded Map growth.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Unit tests cover: under-limit, at-limit, over-limit, proxy header, cleanup
criteriaAssessment.evidence: rateLimit.test.ts contains exactly one test ('allows requests under limit'). At-limit, over-limit, proxy header, and cleanup scenarios have no test coverage.
